policy: Exactly these five origins are supplied to Trystero. Public invitation fragments cannot add or replace relays.
test_method: Release qualification uses two independent bounded clients: client A subscribes to a fresh random topic, client B publishes a signed EVENT, the relay must return OK true to B and deliver the exact event ID/content to A, then both send CLOSE and disconnect. Open/EOSE alone does not qualify signaling. CI is offline and does not run this network test; runtime health separately requires observed EVENT acceptance and subscribed delivery.
service_level: Best effort public infrastructure. The project has no ownership, contract, or SLA; there is no SLA.
privacy: Relay operators can observe connecting IP addresses, relay URLs, timing, ephemeral Nostr public keys, event IDs/signatures, created_at, kind, and the plaintext x tag containing a deterministic topic hash. EVENT content carries Trystero-encrypted signaling. Invited peers can still cause pre-auth WebRTC candidate exchange and may learn candidate metadata. Media and application actions use direct WebRTC.
